description_long: This is a hardcover book with a predominantly white and black cover, featuring a technical illustration of a surveyor operating a tripod-mounted instrument, accompanied by two vertical measuring rods. The book is rectangular, with a moderate thickness and a sturdy, slightly glossy cover. The cover art is rendered in a sketch-like, monochrome style, giving it a professional and academic appearance. The spine is clearly labeled and matches the cover's color scheme, with the title and author information running horizontally. The edges of the book are clean and the corners are slightly rounded, typical of a well-bound hardcover. The book appears to be in good condition, with no visible damage or excessive wear.
description: A hardcover book with a white and black cover featuring a technical illustration of a surveyor and measuring rods, giving it a professional, academic look. The book is rectangular, moderately thick, and in good condition.
description_view_0: The front cover is visible, showing the technical illustration and the full title clearly.
description_view_1: The book is viewed from the side, displaying the spine with the title and author information, and the thickness of the book.
description_view_2: The back cover is partially visible, with the book's thickness and the edge of the cover art apparent.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the spine is shown, with the cover art wrapping around and the book's rectangular shape emphasized.
attribute_cover_illustration: The front cover features a monochrome, sketch-style illustration of a surveyor operating a tripod-mounted instrument, with two vertical measuring rods positioned beside the figure. The artwork is technical and precise, emphasizing the subject matter related to surveying.
attribute_title_text: Principles and Use of Surveying Instruments
attribute_spine_design: The spine is white with black text running horizontally, displaying the title and author information. The design is simple and matches the academic style of the cover.
